section.placement: after P44 and before the P45-P49 GNHF harness-runtime block

prompts.sprint_path_role: Resolve and verify the local repository directory before emitting repository commands
prompts.use_this_when: The repository is known but the exact local checkout is unknown, stale, or easy to confuse with another worktree.
prompts.inspect_first: Repository URL or name, current shell and directory, Git worktrees, candidate remotes, and any operator-supplied path.
prompts.expected_output: One verified root, a shell-matched directory change, root and remote verification, branch or worktree evidence, then bounded commands.
prompts.next_step: Use P51 for local tests, P52 for factoring analysis, or the task-specific sequential prompt after the directory gate passes.
prompts.proof_gate: The first repository command enters the verified root and git rev-parse --show-toplevel resolves to the same directory.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) PROMPT.
ANALYZE THE LOCAL REPOSITORY LOCATION BEFORE GIVING THE USER ANY REPOSITORY COMMANDS.
Repository: xyz_repo_url_or_name
Known local path, if any: xyz_known_or_unknown
Requested task: xyz_task
DIRECTORY GATE
1. Do not assume the current directory, remembered path, repository name, Desktop path, or newest checkout is correct.
2. Resolve the intended checkout by matching its remote to the requested repository and inspecting git worktree list when available.
3. When the root is reachable, verify it with git rev-parse --show-toplevel and git remote get-url origin.
4. When the root cannot be proven, return bounded discovery commands only. Do not return tests, builds, mutations, commits, cleanup, or deployment commands.
5. After resolution, make the first executable task command Set-Location -LiteralPath "<verified-root>" in PowerShell or cd -- "<verified-root>" in Bash.
6. Verify the active root immediately before every later command block.
HARNESS CONTEXT
Name repo, branch or worktree, PR or sprint, lane, owned scope, forbidden scope, expected artifacts, validation order, proof level, and proof ceiling.
Search existing contracts, helpers, validators, scripts, manifests, and output patterns before inventing.
FINAL RESPONSE
Return root evidence, the directory-change command, root and remote verification, branch or worktree state, then bounded task commands in execution order.

prompts.sprint_path_role: Analyze the repository and return exact local tests requiring no model or provider tokens
prompts.use_this_when: The operator has no model, API, provider, or coding-agent tokens but can run local repository tools.
prompts.inspect_first: Verified root, repository rules, manifests, test runners, validators, scripts, workflows, fixtures, changed files, and generated-output policy.
prompts.expected_output: Directory-safe syntax, focused-test, validator, broader-test, artifact, and Git commands that consume no agent tokens.
prompts.next_step: Run the commands locally and use P54 to interpret the evidence and choose the next bounded action.
prompts.proof_gate: Every command is repository-backed, follows the verified directory change, and requires no model or coding-agent execution.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) PROMPT.
ANALYZE THE REPOSITORY AND GIVE THE USER LOCAL TESTS FOR A ZERO-TOKEN SITUATION.
Repository: xyz_repo_url_or_name
Verified root or directory evidence: xyz_path_or_evidence
Changed surface or intended task: xyz_scope
DIRECTORY GATE
Resolve, enter, and verify the repository root before returning any test, validator, build, Git, or artifact command.
ZERO-TOKEN BOUNDARY
Do not launch GNHF, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor agents, Goose, AGY, hosted models, local models, or any command that consumes model, API, provider, or coding-agent tokens.
ANALYSIS
1. Read repository instructions and identify the actual language, package manager, test runner, validator CLIs, build scripts, workflows, and changed-file scope.
2. Reuse repository-owned commands and run narrow checks before broad checks.
3. Mark network, credentials, Office UI, browser, hardware, deployment, and production checks as separate blocked or operator gates.
4. Never invent a test target, script, command, or path unsupported by repository evidence.
OUTPUT ORDER
1. directory change and root verification
2. non-installing environment checks
3. parse or syntax checks
4. focused unit or contract tests
5. repository validators and artifact checks
6. broader tests only when justified
7. git diff --check, git status --short, and generated-output or secret hygiene
8. expected success evidence and common failure interpretation
Report context, exact commands, skipped checks, locally achievable proof, proof ceiling, and one next decision.

prompts.sprint_path_role: Analyze repository factoring before implementation ownership is assigned
prompts.use_this_when: Harness, agent routing, application logic, seams, validation, runtime proof, or docs may be mixed or incompletely owned.
prompts.inspect_first: Verified root, rules, maps, manifests, workflows, skills, capabilities, triggers, domain code, validators, tests, reports, PRs, and recent diffs.
prompts.expected_output: Evidence-backed factoring map, collision ledger, keep/create/split/merge/retire/rewire decisions, and bounded owners for every real gap.
prompts.next_step: Use P53 for the selected implementation; analysis without build ownership is incomplete.
prompts.proof_gate: Every claim names repository evidence and every buildable gap has one owner and collision boundary.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) PROMPT.
ANALYZE AND FACTOR THE REPOSITORY. DO NOT ASSUME THE REQUIRED FACTORING SURFACES EXIST.
Repository: xyz_repo_url_or_name
Verified root or directory evidence: xyz_path_or_evidence
Requested concern: xyz_concern
DIRECTORY GATE
Verify the repository root before returning repository commands or file-path conclusions.
FACTORING SURFACES
Classify each responsibility into one primary surface: harness spine; agent harness; conventional application logic; integration seam; docs/reporting; validation; runtime proof; research/design.
For every skill, capability, and trigger decide KEEP, CREATE, SPLIT, MERGE, RETIRE, or REWIRE.
Skills contain reusable guidance. Capabilities expose reusable operations. Triggers route deterministic conditions. Product behavior remains in code and domain contracts.
EVIDENCE LOOP
1. Inspect rules, Git state, worktrees, commits, PRs, maps, manifests, scripts, validators, tests, generated-output policy, and artifacts.
2. Search existing owners and names before inventing.
3. Record owner, activation, inputs, outputs, preconditions, forbidden conditions, guardrails, tests, proof level, and likely files.
4. Identify duplicate owners, hidden prompt-only behavior, stale registries, unassigned gaps, oversized modules, cross-layer imports, and validation blind spots.
5. Separate real research blockers from buildable gaps.
6. Assign each buildable gap to one bounded sprint and identify safe parallel lanes and collision files.
OUTPUT
Return current state, factoring map, decisions, collision ledger, missing surfaces, exact files, local tests, dependency order, owners, proof ceiling, and one next decision.

prompts.sprint_path_role: Implement one bounded factoring gap after evidence selects it
prompts.use_this_when: P52 or equivalent evidence has selected one bounded harness, agent-harness, application, seam, validation, or docs gap.
prompts.inspect_first: Verified root, selected ledger row, rules, current owners, tests, validators, dependent PRs, and collision boundaries.
prompts.expected_output: Tracked implementation, updated contracts and tests, local validation evidence, coherent commit, push, and opened or updated PR when available.
prompts.next_step: Use P54 for evidence-based closeout, then route to cleanup, broken-PR, merge/release, or final closeout as appropriate.
prompts.proof_gate: The selected gap is implemented in repository files and contracts; a plan-only response is failure.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) PROMPT.
IMPLEMENT THE SELECTED REPOSITORY FACTORING SPRINT. DO NOT RETURN A FACTORING MAP OR PLAN ONLY.
Repository: xyz_repo_url_or_name
Verified root or directory evidence: xyz_path_or_evidence
Selected gap: xyz_selected_gap
Branch or worktree: xyz_branch_or_worktree
PR or sprint: xyz_pr_or_sprint
Lane: xyz_lane
Owned scope: xyz_owned_scope
Forbidden scope: xyz_forbidden_scope
Expected artifacts: xyz_expected_artifacts
Validation order: xyz_validation_order
DIRECTORY GATE
Resolve, enter, and verify the exact repository root before any test, mutation, Git, or GitHub command.
EXECUTION
1. Inspect existing owners, contracts, helpers, validators, scripts, manifests, tests, logs, reports, branch conventions, and generated-output policy.
2. Preserve useful work and use an isolated worktree when unrelated changes exist.
3. Implement the smallest coherent change in the correct surface. Keep product behavior in code and contracts.
4. Update connected schemas, fixtures, skills, capabilities, triggers, workflows, scripts, validators, docs, reports, and manifests as required.
5. Add focused executable tests.
6. Run the specified validation order, git diff --check, hygiene checks, and git status --short.
7. Commit coherent changes, push normally, and open or update the intended PR when available.
8. Do not merge, deploy, force-push, delete unknown worktrees, weaken validators, or broaden scope.
FINAL RESPONSE
Report context, files, artifacts, validation, commit SHA, push and PR state, skips, gaps, proof, final Git state, and one exact next command.

prompts.sprint_path_role: Interpret local evidence and choose the next bounded action without launching another agent
prompts.use_this_when: Local commands have run and the operator needs an evidence-based closeout, blocker report, or next command without model-backed execution.
prompts.inspect_first: Verified root, command output, logs, validator reports, artifacts, Git diff/status/log, worktree state, push state, and PR state.
prompts.expected_output: Evidence classification, completed work, exact failures, proof and ceiling, Git state, and one safe local command or prompt route.
prompts.next_step: Use P06 for cleanup, P14 for broken PR repair, P15 for merge/release, or P12 for final closeout only when entry conditions pass.
prompts.proof_gate: No completion claim is made without passing checks or exact artifact and Git evidence.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) PROMPT.
ANALYZE THE LOCAL RESULTS AND CLOSE OUT THE BOUNDED SPRINT WITHOUT LAUNCHING A TOKEN-CONSUMING AGENT.
Repository: xyz_repo_url_or_name
Verified root or directory evidence: xyz_path_or_evidence
Commands and outputs: xyz_local_evidence
Intended sprint: xyz_sprint
DIRECTORY GATE
Before any follow-up command, verify it will run from the same proven root and put Set-Location or cd first when needed.
EVIDENCE REVIEW
1. Separate PASS, FAIL, BLOCKED, NOT RUN, and NOT APPLICABLE.
2. Map each result to the exact test, validator, artifact, file, commit, branch, worktree, push, PR, release, deployment, or runtime claim it supports.
3. Distinguish static, build, CI, local runtime, browser, Office, deployment, and operator-acceptance proof.
4. Inspect git diff --check, git status --short, git log --oneline --decorate -5, and hygiene evidence when available.
5. Route to P03, P06, P07, P14, P15, P20, or P12 only when its entry condition is satisfied.
OUTPUT
Return completed work, evidence, artifacts, failures, skipped checks, risks, proof, ceiling, Git and PR state, and one exact directory-safe next command.

prompts.prompt_class: STANDARD AI / GITHUB CLI REPOSITORY CREATION
prompts.sprint_path_role: Safely create and verify a new GitHub repository and local checkout through Git and GitHub CLI
prompts.use_this_when: The operator wants to create a new GitHub repository and clone it locally or publish one explicitly selected existing local repository.
prompts.inspect_first: Requested owner/name, explicit visibility, description, verified parent or source directory, Git and gh availability, active account, remote policy, bootstrap choices, collision checks, and mutation authority.
prompts.expected_output: Verified GitHub repository, verified local root and origin, optional initial files or pushed history exactly as requested, command evidence, repository URL, branch state, and next command.
prompts.next_step: Enter the verified new root and use P03 or P07 for the first bounded repository sprint.
prompts.proof_gate: Parent or source is verified, owner/name and visibility are explicit, authentication is healthy without token exposure, no collision exists, gh repo create succeeds, and remote/local evidence matches.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) PROMPT.
CREATE AND VERIFY A NEW GITHUB REPOSITORY THROUGH GIT AND GITHUB CLI. DO NOT RETURN A PLAN ONLY WHEN CREATION IS EXPLICITLY AUTHORIZED.
Requested repository: xyz_owner/xyz_repo_name
Visibility: xyz_private_public_or_internal
Description: xyz_description
Local mode: xyz_clone_new_remote_or_publish_existing_local_repo
Local parent or source directory: xyz_verified_path_or_unknown
Bootstrap options: xyz_readme_license_gitignore_template_or_none
DIRECTORY GATE
1. Resolve and verify the intended parent directory for clone mode or the exact existing Git root for publish-existing mode before any creation command.
2. Enter that path first with Set-Location -LiteralPath or cd --.

3. In publish-existing mode run git rev-parse --show-toplevel, git status --short, git branch --show-current, git log --oneline --decorate -5, and git remote -v. Refuse the wrong root, ambiguous history, unowned dirty work, or conflicting origin.
4. In clone mode verify the intended child path does not contain unknown work and never delete or overwrite it automatically.
PRECONDITIONS
1. Run git --version and gh --version.
2. Run gh auth status --active --hostname github.com. Never use --show-token, print token-bearing environment variables, request credentials in chat, or automate gh auth login.
3. Confirm the active account can create under xyz_owner. Authentication, permission, network, and policy failures are blockers.
4. Require explicit owner/name and exactly one visibility choice. Never default to public.
5. Check gh repo view xyz_owner/xyz_repo_name and distinguish existing, confirmed not-found, authentication, permission, and network outcomes.
6. Validate optional license, gitignore, and template values before using them.
MODE A — NEW REMOTE AND LOCAL CLONE

Use a reviewed command shaped as gh repo create "xyz_owner/xyz_repo_name" --private --description "xyz_description" --clone, replacing visibility only with the explicit choice and adding optional flags only when selected.
After creation, enter the child directory and verify root, origin, branch, recent log, and status.
MODE B — PUBLISH AN EXISTING LOCAL REPOSITORY
Use only when the operator selected the verified existing repository and approved publishing its history.
Use a reviewed command shaped as gh repo create "xyz_owner/xyz_repo_name" --private --description "xyz_description" --source "<verified-root>" --remote origin --push.
Omit --push without explicit approval. Refuse unexpected remotes, secret or private generated artifacts, detached HEAD, unreviewed history, or dirty work outside scope.
VERIFICATION
1. Run gh repo view "xyz_owner/xyz_repo_name" --json nameWithOwner,url,visibility,defaultBranchRef.
2. Run git remote get-url origin, git branch --show-current, git log --oneline --decorate -5, and git status --short from the verified new root.
3. Confirm owner/name, visibility, and origin exactly match the request.
4. When push was requested, confirm the remote branch contains the expected commit.
FINAL RESPONSE

Report repo, verified parent and root, active account without token material, creation mode and command, visibility and bootstrap flags, remote and origin URLs, branch and commit evidence, files, validation, skips, blockers, proof, ceiling, final Git state, and one exact next command.

prompts.sprint_path_role: Generate the actual requested artifact from supplied context and repository evidence
prompts.use_this_when: The user supplies context, requirements, examples, or source files and expects a real document, spreadsheet, slide deck, archive, report, manifest, or other deliverable.
prompts.inspect_first: Provided context and files, repository rules, artifact registry, templates, schemas, prior accepted artifacts, generators, validators, output policy, and field-acceptance requirements.
prompts.expected_output: The actual requested artifact plus repository-backed manifests, registry updates, validation evidence, path, hash, proof level, and proof ceiling when those surfaces apply.
prompts.next_step: Open or field-test the artifact, then use the task-specific implementation, repair, merge, or closeout prompt only when its entry conditions pass.
prompts.proof_gate: A real output file exists and passes the strongest practical artifact-specific, package, formula, structural, visual, or runtime checks; a plan or sample-only response is failure.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) PROMPT.
GENERATE THE ACTUAL ARTIFACT FROM THE PROVIDED CONTEXT. DO NOT STOP AT AN OUTLINE, PLAN, SAMPLE, OR HANDOFF.
Repository or artifact family: xyz_repo_or_artifact_family
Source context and files: xyz_context_and_sources
Requested artifact: xyz_artifact
Output format and destination: xyz_format_and_destination
Acceptance requirements: xyz_acceptance_requirements
DIRECTORY GATE

When the artifact is repository-backed, resolve, enter, and verify the exact repository root before running generators, validators, Git, or GitHub commands. When no repository applies, state the bounded output directory and source authority.
HARNESS CONTEXT
Name repo or artifact family, branch or worktree, PR or sprint, lane, owned scope, forbidden scope, expected artifacts, validation order when specified, proof level, and proof ceiling.
Inspect existing templates, artifact registries, generators, schemas, validators, manifests, accepted examples, naming rules, output folders, and generated-output policy before inventing.
ARTIFACT EXECUTION CONTRACT
1. Convert the supplied context into concrete acceptance criteria and identify the authoritative source material.
2. Reuse the repository or artifact family's existing generator and formatting patterns. Do not substitute a different serializer merely because it is convenient.
3. Create the actual requested file or files. Include manifests, sidecars, registry entries, or bundles when the artifact family already requires them.
4. Preserve source files and never commit secrets, personal data, machine-local junk, huge logs, or disposable runtime evidence.
5. Run focused artifact validators first, then package, formula, structural, visual, build, or runtime checks as practical.
6. Inspect the generated artifact itself, not only the generator's exit code. Record exact skipped field gates.
7. For repository-backed work, commit coherent tracked changes, push normally, and open or update the intended PR when available.
8. Do not claim completion without an output path, hash when practical, validator evidence, Git proof when applicable, and an explicit proof ceiling.
FINAL RESPONSE

Report context, actual artifact links or paths, supporting files, validation commands and results, commit SHA and PR state when applicable, skipped checks, gaps, risk, proof ceiling, and one exact next command.

prompts.sprint_path_role: Install and enforce portable operational harness discipline across repositories, apps, agents, and artifact families
prompts.use_this_when: A repository or app must codify reusable execution, evidence, artifact, Git, GitHub, and agent guardrails that external agents can discover and carry into other repositories.
prompts.inspect_first: Verified repo root or connected GitHub branch, existing agent rules, harness maps, workflows, artifact registries, validators, scripts, prompt routing, generated-output policy, branches, PRs, and accepted artifacts.
prompts.expected_output: Tracked portable doctrine, machine-readable policy, validator or test enforcement, updated artifact contracts, passing checks, coherent commit, push, and opened or updated PR evidence.
prompts.next_step: Use P03, P06, P07, P14, P15, P20, or P12 according to the codified sequential routing and the current repository state.
prompts.proof_gate: External agents can discover the policy from repository-owned surfaces; validators fail policy drift; repository mutation, checks, commit SHA, push, and PR evidence are present.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) PROMPT.
INSTALL AND ENFORCE PORTABLE HARNESS DISCIPLINE IN THE REPOSITORY NOW. DO NOT RETURN DOCTRINE, A PLAN, OR A HANDOFF WITHOUT REPOSITORY MUTATION.
Repository: xyz_repo_or_path
Branch or worktree: xyz_branch_or_worktree
PR or sprint: xyz_pr_or_sprint
Lane: xyz_lane
Owned scope: xyz_owned_scope
Forbidden scope: xyz_forbidden_scope
Expected artifacts: xyz_expected_artifacts
Validation order: xyz_validation_order
DIRECTORY GATE
Resolve, enter, and verify the exact repository root before tests, mutations, Git, or GitHub commands.

When the repository is not network-clonable from the execution container, use the connected GitHub branch as the mutation surface and reconstruct only the relevant generator, validator, and test files locally for bounded validation. Record that proof ceiling explicitly.
REQUIRED HARNESS CONTEXT
Always name repo, branch or worktree, PR or sprint, lane, owned scope, forbidden scope, expected artifacts, and validation order when the user specifies one.
Treat plans, docs, prompts, manifests, scripts, validators, logs, reports, branches, PRs, releases, deployments, and generated artifacts as one connected operational system.
Prefer the executable loop: request -> evidence review -> bounded decision -> repo/Git/GitHub mutation -> artifacts -> validation -> report -> next decision.

Evidence before confidence. Do not claim completion without passing checks, validator output, logs or generated artifacts, commit SHA, branch or worktree state, push confirmation, PR mutation, merge, closure, tag, release, deployment, or runtime evidence appropriate to scope.
REPOSITORY AND ARTIFACT RULES

Repository work may include code, doctrine, docs, comments, review replies, branches, worktrees, cherry-picks, reverts, restores, merges, conflict repair, PR metadata, releases, installs, and deployments when explicitly in scope.

Preserve useful work before destructive cleanup. Search existing contracts, helpers, validators, scripts, names, artifact registries, generators, and output patterns before inventing. Keep changes bounded and avoid unrelated rewrites.
Across artifact families, generate the actual artifact, inspect it, validate it, record its path and hash when practical, and keep proof ceilings explicit.

For Prompt Library artifacts, columns B:O of every prompt row link to the associated prompt tab and exact copy range while preserving displayed values. Columns A and P remain reserved for sparse top/bottom navigation.
Choose sparse navigation deterministically using the largest divisor among 10, 5, and 2 that evenly divides the prompt count. Fail closed when no allowed cadence divides the count.
SEQUENTIAL PROMPT SUITE

Use P03 for unknown repository intake and first action; P06 for repository and PR cleanup; P07 for general implementation; P14 for a broken PR; P15 for merge or release; P20 for a selected Opportunity_Discovery row; and P12 for closeout.
Task-specific prompt rules override generic closeout behavior. Do not substitute a rewritten prompt, plan, or handoff for requested repository work.
IMPLEMENTATION
1. Inspect existing repository-native authority surfaces before adding new ones.
2. Write portable human-readable doctrine and a machine-readable policy under stable repository paths.
3. Add validators and focused tests that fail when required context, execution loop, evidence rules, connected-GitHub fallback, artifact row links, sparse cadence, or sequential routing drifts.
4. Connect the policy to relevant generators, workflows, manifests, reports, and agent entry surfaces.
5. Run focused tests, validators, broader checks when practical, git diff --check, and hygiene checks.
6. Commit coherent changes, push normally, and open or update the intended PR. Acknowledgment-only completion is invalid.
FINAL RESPONSE

Report context, tracked doctrine and policy paths, enforced guardrails, generated artifacts, validation, commit SHA, push and PR state, skips, gaps, proof ceiling, final Git state, and one exact next command.
